sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחְפֹּר, Arabic جَفَرَ-

(1) to dig, as a well, a pit, Gen 21:30 26:15 , seq.; Ecc 10:8 spoken of a horse, Job 39:21, יַחְפְּרוּ בָעֵמֶק “they dig in the valley.” Virg. Georg. 3:87, 88, “cavat tellurem.” (In the Western languages this power is found in the same letters transposed in the roots grf, glf, as γράφω, χρίμπτω; γλάφω, γλύφω; sCRiBo, sCaLPo, sCuLPo; graben.) Metaph. to dig a pit for, to lay snares, to plot, Psa 35:7.

(2) to search out, to explore (comp. בָּקַר No. 3, and Sim. Arc. Form., page 62). Job 39:29, “from thence she seeketh the prey;” followed by an acc. to explore (a country), Deu 1:22 Jos 2:2, Jos 2:3. As to Isa 2:20, see חֲפַרְפֵּרָה .

II.
